title: Microformats Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential class-based semantics for creating structured data in your HTML.

questions.question: What is 'Microformats' in the context of HTML?
options: A way to resize images automatically.
A set of class-based semantic data used to add meaning to existing HTML elements.
A special CSS property for applying animations.
An automated script used specifically for form validation.

questions.question: Which Microformat is used specifically for representing people, companies, and organizations?
options: hCard
hCalendar
hReview
hPerson

questions.question: What does the 'hCalendar' Microformat allow you to represent on a web page?
options: The page creation date.
Events and schedules (based on iCalendar standards).
The user's local timezone.
The total time a user spends on the site.

questions.question: In Microformats2, what prefix is used to identify a specific property of an object (like a name or email)?
options: prop-*

questions.question: What is a key difference between Microformats and Microdata (Schema.org)?
options: Microformats use external files, while Microdata is inside the HTML.
Microformats rely on the existing 'class' attribute, while Microdata uses new tags like 'itemscope'.
Microdata is only for images, and Microformats are only for text.
There is no difference between them; they are two names for the same thing.
